studyName: Intraductal Transplantation Models of PDAC Reveal Progression of Subtypes

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample IDs, body site, xenograft body site, control status, analyte type, histological type, tumor status, sample type, engraftment model, project analysis, intraductally grafted organoid (IGO) progressor type, KRAS in vivo, KRAS in vitro, isolated region, and days of harvested sample.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte type of sample

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Site in the patient's body where the specimen used to generate the organoid line was collected

variables.name: CLONE_ID
variables.description: For samples derived from clonal organoids or xenografts of clonal organoids, the clone ID of the organoid

variables.name: CONTROL_STATUS
variables.description: For experiments that compared treated cells or mice to controls (project analyses 4 and 5 detailed below), this describes whether the sample was a case or a control

variables.name: DAY_SAMPLE_HARVESTED
variables.description: The number of days after initial transplant that the tumor was harvested, or the number of days after plating that the organoids were harvested

variables.name: ENGRAFTMENT_MODEL
variables.description: Method used to engraft patient-derived organoid cells into the pancreas of NSG (NOD.Cg-Prkdcscid Il2rgtm1Wjl/SzJ) mice

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Histology of sample

variables.name: IGO_PROGRESSOR_TYPE
variables.description: For tumors arising from intraductally grafted organoids (IGOs) that were used to compare fast- vs. slow-progessing IGO tumors (i.e. tumors used to generate samples used for Project Analyses 2 or 3), the relative speed of progression

variables.name: ISOLATED_REGION
variables.description: Region isolated by laser-capture microdissection of IGO-derived tumors used for Project Analyses 6, 7, and 8

variables.name: IS_TUMOR
variables.description: Tumor status of sample

variables.name: KRAS_IN_VITRO
variables.description: Treatment of the organoids used for comparison of cultured organoids in which oncogenic KRAS was hyperactivated vs untreated, control organoids for Project Analysis 5

variables.name: KRAS_IN_VIVO
variables.description: Vector construct present in the organoids used to generate the IGO tumors used to compare laser-capture microdissected KRAS-hyperactivated or control regions for Project Analysis 4

variables.name: ORGANOID_USED
variables.description: Organoid line used to generate this sample

variables.name: PROJECT_ANALYSIS
variables.description: Type of analysis in which the sample was included

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: De-identified Sample ID

variables.name: SAMPLE_TYPE
variables.description: Preclinical model system used to generate sample. For samples with SAMPLE_TYPE=ORGANOID, the name of the organoid used to generate the sample. For samples with SAMPLE_TYPE=IGO_LCM or XENOGRAFT, the name of the organoid transplanted to generate the xenograft tumor.

variables.name: XENOGRAFT_BODY_SITE
variables.description: For xenografts of patient-derived organoids, the site in the mouse's body from which the sample was collected

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject IDs, case control status of the subject for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC), sex, age, and race.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of the subject for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC)

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: Age at diagnosis

variables.name: RACE
variables.description: Race of participant

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Sex of participant

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject IDs and consent information.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
